app.title: Refrain Sheet
app.subtitle: A local-first, format-preserving CSV and spreadsheet editor.
tabs.label: Open files
grid.label: CSV grid
grid.empty: No file is open. Use File > Open (Ctrl+O) or drop CSV files anywhere in this window.
welcome.drop: …or drag and drop CSV / RSF files anywhere in this window — each file opens in its own tab.
welcome.offline: Runs fully offline from this single local HTML file: files are opened from and saved to your device, nothing is uploaded, and no network access is needed. Language, spreadsheet font, and the file-size limit are kept in your browser's local settings.
grid.loadMore: Showing {shown} of {total} rows — scroll down to load more
formulaBar.label: Cell value
formulaBar.hint: Enter: apply and move down · Alt+Enter: insert newline · Esc: cancel · Start with = for a formula (RSF)
grid.refsBeyond: A referenced range extends beyond the visible area
formula.fn.SUM: Adds numbers and numeric cells in the ranges.
formula.fn.AVERAGE: Averages the numeric values in the ranges.
formula.fn.MIN: Smallest numeric value in the ranges.
formula.fn.MAX: Largest numeric value in the ranges.
formula.fn.COUNT: Counts the numeric values in the ranges.
formula.fn.IF: Returns one value if the condition is true, another if false.
dialog.formulaHelp.title: Formula and Function Help
dialog.formulaHelp.intro: Formulas begin with = and are evaluated entirely offline in this page. Search below, or browse the reference.
dialog.formulaHelp.noResults: No help entries match your search.
dialog.formulaHelp.syntaxBody: A formula starts with = followed by an expression, for example =A1+B1*2. Text values use double quotes ("like this"). Whitespace is ignored. Formulas are a spreadsheet (RSF) feature; entering one in a plain CSV offers to convert the document.
dialog.formulaHelp.referencesBody: Refer to a cell by its column letters and 1-based row number. References are relative: copying or filling a formula shifts them by the same offset. Absolute and mixed ($) references are not supported.
dialog.formulaHelp.rangesBody: A rectangular range is two corner references joined by a colon (A1:B10). A:A or A:C covers whole columns; 1:1 or 2:10 covers whole rows. Whole-column and whole-row ranges are bounded to the used grid, never an infinite sheet.
dialog.formulaHelp.operatorsBody: Arithmetic: + - * / with parentheses for grouping. Comparisons = <> < > <= >= return TRUE or FALSE, useful as the condition in IF.
dialog.formulaHelp.errorsIntro: When a formula cannot be evaluated it shows an error code as its value:
dialog.formulaHelp.err.error: The formula is not valid (a syntax error).
dialog.formulaHelp.err.name: An unknown function or name was used.
dialog.formulaHelp.err.value: A value has the wrong type (for example adding text), or a range was used where a single value is required.
dialog.formulaHelp.err.div0: Division by zero (including AVERAGE of no numbers).
dialog.formulaHelp.err.ref: A reference points to a cell that was deleted or is otherwise invalid.
dialog.formulaHelp.err.cycle: A circular reference: a formula depends on its own result, directly or indirectly.
dialog.formulaHelp.autocompleteBody: While typing a function name a suggestion list appears; press Tab or Enter to accept, or Esc to dismiss. While editing a formula, click a cell to insert its reference, or drag across cells to insert a range.
find.aborted: Search stopped: time limit exceeded (partial results shown)
dialog.validation.title: CSV Validation Results
dialog.validation.intro: Structural problems were found in "{name}". Nothing has been repaired or normalized; if you open the file, all malformed regions are preserved byte-for-byte until you edit them.
diagDesc.unclosed-quote: A quoted field is never closed; the rest of the file is treated as part of this field.
diagDesc.text-after-quote: Extra text appears between a closing quote and the next delimiter.
diagDesc.bare-quote: A double quote appears inside an unquoted field.
diagDesc.inconsistent-field-count: Expected {expected} fields (as in row 1) but found {actual}.
diagDesc.ambiguous: Line breaks inside an unclosed quote make the remaining structure unreliable.
dialog.saveOptions.title: Save with Options
dialog.saveOptions.reencodeWarning: Choosing a different encoding re-encodes the entire document. Field quoting, whitespace, delimiters, and record structure are preserved where possible, but byte-for-byte preservation of unmodified fields no longer applies.
dialog.saveOptions.injectionWarning: Note: values beginning with =, +, -, or @ may be interpreted as formulas by spreadsheet software. Refrain Sheet never modifies your values to mitigate this.
dialog.unsaved.title: Unsaved Changes
dialog.unrepresentable.title: Characters Cannot Be Represented
dialog.unrepresentable.message: The selected encoding ({encoding}) cannot represent some characters. Saving has been cancelled.
dialog.unrepresentable.continueNcr: Save Anyway (replace with numeric character references such as 😀)
dialog.ncrDone.title: Characters Were Replaced
dialog.ncrDone.message: {count} characters in {cells} cells were replaced with numeric character references: {list}
dialog.undecodableEdit.title: Edited Cells Contained Undecodable Bytes
dialog.undecodableEdit.message: These edited cells originally contained bytes that could not be decoded: {list}. Saving will replace those original bytes with the edited text. Unmodified cells keep their original bytes.
dialog.reopen.title: Reopen with Encoding
dialog.reopen.warning: Reopening reinterprets the same original bytes with the selected encoding and delimiter. The file's bytes are never modified. Current unsaved edits will be discarded.
dialog.unsupported.title: Possibly Unsupported Encoding
dialog.unsupported.message: "{name}" may use {candidate}, which is not supported. Supported encodings are UTF-8 (with or without BOM), Shift_JIS / CP932, and EUC-JP. The file will be opened as {encoding}; undecodable bytes are shown as replacement characters but are preserved on save unless their cell is edited.
dialog.uncertain.message: The encoding of "{name}" could not be detected reliably. It will be opened as Shift_JIS / CP932. Use File > Reopen with Encoding… to change the interpretation.
dialog.nonCsv.title: Not a CSV File?
dialog.nonCsv.message: "{name}" does not have a typical CSV extension (.csv, .tsv, .txt). It will be opened as CSV text if you continue.
dialog.tooLarge.title: File Too Large
dialog.tooLarge.message: "{name}" is {size} MiB, over the current {limit} MiB limit. Refrain Sheet keeps the whole file in memory. You can raise the limit in Settings, but very large files may still fail to load or be slow to render and edit.
dialog.settings.title: Settings
dialog.settings.note: Raising the limit does not guarantee a file will open: browser memory, device resources, and file complexity still impose practical limits.
dialog.settings.local: This setting is stored only in your browser and is never transmitted anywhere.
dialog.about.title: About Refrain Sheet
dialog.about.tagline: A local-first, format-preserving CSV and spreadsheet editor.
dialog.about.body: Refrain Sheet edits CSV field values while preserving everything else byte-for-byte where possible: delimiters, quoting, whitespace, line endings, encodings, BOMs, undecodable bytes, and malformed regions. Lightweight spreadsheet editing — formulas, structural edits, and metadata — is available through RSF (Refrain Sheet Format). It runs fully offline; no data ever leaves this page.
dialog.about.shortcutsNote: Shortcuts are optional accelerators; every command is also on the menus. They deliberately avoid browser-reserved keys (Ctrl+N/T/W, Ctrl+F, Ctrl+H, Ctrl+Tab, reload, print, zoom, dev tools).
dialog.about.appearanceNote: Theme: choose View > Theme (System default, Light, or Dark). System default follows your OS/browser setting and updates live. While editing a cell or the formula bar, press Alt+Enter to insert a line break within the cell. Japanese, Chinese, and Korean IME input is fully supported — start typing to begin composition in the cell.
shortcut.findNextPrev: Find next / previous (in the Find bar)
drop.hint: Drop CSV files to open them — each file opens in its own tab
notify.savedOverwrite: Saved: the original file was overwritten in place.
notify.savedDownload: Saved as a download "{name}". The browser chooses the download location; the original file was NOT overwritten.
notify.permissionDenied: Write permission was denied; falling back to a download save.
notify.sameFile: "{name}" is already open; switched to its tab.
notify.settingsSaved: Settings saved. Maximum file size: {size} MiB.
loading.wrapMeasure: Measuring wrapped row heights — {done} of {total} rows ({pct}%)
save.downloadNote: This browser or context cannot overwrite the original file directly. The save will be downloaded as "{name}" instead.
grid.resizeTitle: Drag to resize; double-click to auto-fit (grows or shrinks to fit content)
grid.autoFitSampled: Auto-fit column {letter} used the {n} visible rows (large sheet). Scroll to include other rows and re-fit if needed.
grid.autoFitSampledMulti: Auto-fit measured a sample of rows in {n} column(s) (large sheet). Scroll to include other rows and re-fit if needed.
grid.fillTitle: Drag to fill values and formulas into adjacent cells
grid.stickyRowTitle: First record row (pinned). This is document data, not a column header.
status.compressionTitle: Compression method used for the .rsf container. Change it in File > Save with Options…
status.engineTitle: CSV parsing engine: WASM is the embedded Rust core; JS is the fallback with identical behavior.
rsf.method.zstd.label: Zstandard (Recommended)
rsf.method.zstd.desc: Balanced compression ratio and speed. The default for new .rsf documents.
rsf.method.lz4.label: LZ4 Frame (Fast)
rsf.method.lz4.desc: Optimized for fast saving and opening; files may be larger than Zstandard.
rsf.method.deflate.label: DEFLATE (Compatible)
rsf.method.deflate.desc: Widely compatible fallback, used automatically when Zstandard is unavailable.
rsf.method.store.label: None (Uncompressed)
rsf.method.store.desc: No compression. For debugging, interoperability, or troubleshooting only.
dialog.convert.title: Convert to Spreadsheet (RSF)?
dialog.convert.formula: You entered a formula. Formulas cannot be stored in "{name}" without giving up the byte-preservation guarantee, so the document must become an RSF spreadsheet document. If you cancel, the text is kept as a plain value.
dialog.convert.paste: This paste does not fit into the existing rows and columns of "{name}". A byte-preserving CSV document never changes its row/column structure silently, so the document must become an RSF spreadsheet document first.
dialog.convert.structure: Inserting or deleting rows or columns changes the structure of "{name}". A byte-preserving CSV document never changes its structure, so the document must become an RSF spreadsheet document first.
dialog.convert.fill: Filling copies values and formulas across multiple cells of "{name}", a spreadsheet-only operation. A byte-preserving CSV document is never modified this way, so the document must become an RSF spreadsheet document first.
dialog.convert.command: Convert "{name}" to an RSF spreadsheet document. RSF supports formulas, row and column insertion and deletion, range fill, persistent column widths, and spreadsheet metadata. The current (including unsaved) contents of "{name}" are used.
dialog.convert.note: After conversion the tab becomes an .rsf spreadsheet document: formulas and row/column edits are supported, but the byte-level preservation guarantee no longer applies and saving produces an .rsf file. The original CSV file on disk is never modified by the conversion. This tab's undo history is cleared.
dialog.convert.commandNote: The converted spreadsheet opens in a new tab; the original CSV tab and the file on disk stay open and unchanged. Conversion ends the byte-identical CSV round-trip guarantee for the new RSF document, which is saved as an .rsf file.
dialog.rsfSave.title: Saved as RSF
dialog.rsfSave.message: "{name}" is a spreadsheet document (formulas, row/column edits, or spreadsheet metadata). CSV cannot store these without losing the original-file guarantees, so it is saved in the .rsf format — a documented, versioned binary format.
dialog.rsfSave.note: To get a plain CSV file with the calculated values instead, use File > Export as CSV…
dialog.exportCsv.title: Export as CSV (lossy)
dialog.exportCsv.message: "{name}" will be exported as a plain CSV file. CSV export is a lossy conversion from an RSF spreadsheet document.
dialog.exportCsv.warning: Formulas are exported as their calculated display values, not as formula expressions. The .rsf document stays open and unchanged.
dialog.exportCsv.notPreserved: Not preserved in the CSV: formulas and their dependency information, row/column structure beyond the exported grid, column widths, document metadata, font preferences, and other spreadsheet-only data.
dialog.exportCsv.bomNote: A byte order mark applies only to UTF-8; the BOM choice is disabled and ignored for Shift_JIS and EUC-JP.
dialog.insertCells.title: Insert Copied Cells
dialog.insertCells.message: Insert the copied {rows} × {cols} cell range at the selection by shifting existing cells.
dialog.insertCells.note: Shifting cells down inserts whole rows across the sheet; shifting cells right inserts whole columns. This keeps every formula consistent: existing references adjust exactly like Insert Rows/Columns, and relative references in the inserted formulas shift by their offset from the copied location. The insertion is a single undoable operation.
dialog.deleteRows.title: Delete Rows
dialog.deleteRows.message: Delete {n} row(s)? Non-empty cells will be discarded. This can be undone with Ctrl+Z.
dialog.deleteCols.title: Delete Columns
dialog.deleteCols.message: Delete {n} column(s)? Non-empty cells will be discarded. This can be undone with Ctrl+Z.
dialog.rsfInvalid.title: Cannot Open RSF File
dialog.rsfInvalid.message: "{name}" could not be opened as an RSF spreadsheet document: {reason}
dialog.rsfInvalid.badMagic: the file is not a Refrain Sheet document (bad magic bytes).
dialog.rsfInvalid.badVersion: the container version is not supported by this release (expected RSF version 3, or a legacy RCSV version 2 file).
dialog.rsfInvalid.compression: the file uses a compression method this environment cannot read.
dialog.rsfInvalid.tooLarge: the sheet exceeds the supported size limits.
notify.converted: Converted to an RSF spreadsheet document. Save produces an .rsf file; the original CSV on disk is unchanged.
notify.convertedNewTab: Converted to a new RSF spreadsheet tab "{name}". The original CSV tab is unchanged.
notify.rsfMigrated: Opened a legacy .rcsv file as "{name}". Save writes a new .rsf file; the original .rcsv on disk is unchanged.
notify.copied: Copied the selected range to the clipboard.
notify.clipboardBlocked: The browser blocked clipboard access here. Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V still work, and pasting within the app uses the internal clipboard.
notify.pasteBlocked: The browser blocked clipboard reading here. Use Ctrl+V / Cmd+V instead.
notify.cannotDeleteAll: The last remaining rows or columns cannot be deleted.
notify.exportedCsvDownload: Exported the calculated values as a download "{name}".
notify.nothingToInsert: Nothing to insert: copy a cell range first.
notify.insertedCols: Inserted {n} copied column(s) to the left of column {col}.
notify.selectAllEmpty: There are no cells to select in this document.
shortcut.selectAll: Select all cells (only while the grid has focus)
